title: The Silent Current Library

features.name: water-carved shelf
features.desc-on-touch: stone shelves worn into a shape that looks like text, like rows of books arranged by current instead of hands — you run your fingers along the groove and remember a word you never read

features.name: alphabet stones
features.desc-on-touch: pebbles arranged as if someone sorted them by the angle of their edges, by some logic only water understands — each stone holds a letter that the current is teaching you to read

features.name: pooled current
features.desc-on-touch: still water where the alphabet stalls and pools, reflecting sky in a way that reads backward — you see the words your hand is about to write before you write them

features.name: river-whisper glyph
features.desc-on-touch: a mark on the stone that might be natural or written — it hums in a frequency only the water remembers, a frequency lower than language

features.name: current-memory chamber
features.desc-on-touch: a hollow in the stone that catches leaves, insects, small things the river wanted to keep — each visitor to this library leaves a mark that the current is slowly reading back to understand what drowns

inscription: where water learned language and unlearned it again, a library made of current teaching stone to remember without words

file.name: River_Alphabet_Current_Study_Fieldnotes.mp3
file.note: recordings of water moving over stone, slowed down until the flow becomes text — a frequency study of how current speaks what drowning forgets

exits.label: back up the bed where letters are becoming themselves again
deeper into the bedrock where language pools and waits
toward the source where the alphabet was first sung
a route the mesh is still growing
exits.to: NEW: a node the mesh has not decided on
